summary: Claude 接入了 Microsoft 365，Devin 把博客文章直接变成了可运行的代码，Garry Tan 还发布了一个 DX review 工具。Peter Steinberger 说，GitHub 的 API 还是没按 agent 的需求来设计；Aaron Levie 则提醒，agent 最后也会撞上和 manager 一样的墙。

builderInsights.authorBio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8d1e5 or download the app.
builderInsights.title: Claude
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Microsoft 365 connectors 现在对所有 Claude 套餐都开放了。Outlook、OneDrive 和 SharePoint 的内容可以直接进到 chat 里。这一步很实用：Claude 不再只是一个独立 assistant，而是开始真正能在你的 inbox 和 docs 里干活了。

tweets.text:
Microsoft 365 connectors are now available on every Claude plan.

Connect Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint to bring your email, docs, and files into the conversation.

Get started here: https://t.co/EdoQeT8BBN https://t.co/sOrigP41FJ

builderInsights.authorBio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞
builderInsights.title: Peter Steinberger
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: GitHub API 还是没为 agent 设计好
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说自己一直撞上 GitHub API 的 quota 限制。这很直接地说明，这个平台还没为 agentic workflows 做好准备。另一个 thread 里，他还说 Anthropic 的最新动作会伤到整个 ecosystem。不过他也提到 Boris 在尽量把影响压下去，靠 cache 修复来降低 API 成本。

tweets.text:
While I think what Anthropic does is sad for the ecosystem, I wanna give Boris credit for doing what he can to soften the fallout.

Today's release will include some fixes for better cache use, to lower cost for API users. https://t.co/DUZNMjKlJ6

woke up and my mentions are full of these

Both me and @davemorin tried to talk sense into Anthropic, best we managed was delaying this for a week.

Funny how timings match up, first they copy some popular features into their closed harness, then they lock out open source. https://t.co/Mgmv6YmW2B
I keep hitting quota limits from GitHub's API. This hasn't been designed with agents in mind.

builderInsights.authorBio: General Partner @SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder @Bevel_Health | Ex: Early Eng @facebook, CTO @Dropbox, Board @Flipkart | Optimist, Builder, Dad
builderInsights.title: Aditya Agarwal
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: 健康 app 正面硬刚 WHOOP
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，一家市值 $10B 的公司，因为一个 app “看起来和用起来像自己家的”就去起诉，完全是走错了路。与其打官司，他更想把 Bevel Health 做成 WHOOP 的免费替代品，并把这件事定义成一个使命：让更好的健康服务人人都用得上。

tweets.text:
It’s amazing when a $10B company sues you because your app "looks and feels" like theirs
 
Let me give you a tip: Instead of spending 300 bucks on an over-priced @WHOOP , you can get @bevel_health  for free.  

We want to bring great health to *everyone*.

We will build, not resort to lawfare.

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
builderInsights.title: Aaron Levie
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Agent 遇到的还是管理那道坎
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，AI agent 并没有绕开老问题：人类能记住的上下文就这么多，所以才需要 delegation。只要 agent 还做不到自己跟踪状态、清晰升级问题、并接入更大的系统，它们就还是离不开真人盯着。也正因为这样，“jobs disappear” 这个说法有点夸张了。

tweets.text:
“There is a limit on human cognition. Even if you're not reviewing everything they're doing, how much you can hold in your head at one time.”

There’s a reason that at a certain scale, teams of people have a manager, and then there are managers of many teams, and so on. Companies don’t inherently love being inefficient. It’s because eventually you run into the limits of how much context you can hold on to produce useful work, so you have to delegate parts to someone else who can track their sub-context.

In a world where agents don’t need to be prompted or have their work reviewed, or where the agent can know perfectly when to escalate when something is going wrong, then agents can completely break free of these context limits of humans. 

But for now, agents are generally only as effective as the context they’re provided, the tools they have access to, the human’s ability to keep them on track or review their work, and incorporate that work into a broader system. 

For now, that will continue to take real (mental) work from the people managing agents. This is also generally why the jobs arguments from those who think people go away will be wrong.

builderInsights.authorBio: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder @garryslist—Creator of GStack—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
builderInsights.title: Garry Tan
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: YC 负责人发布 DX review 工具
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他在 GStack 上发布了 /plan-devex-review，说自己一向先给自己造工具，现在把它分享出来，帮团队做出更好的 developer experience。它的灵感来自 Addy Osmani 的 DX framework，所以这更像一个实用、带明确观点的 workflow tool，而不是又一个泛泛的 AI wrapper。

tweets.text: Garry’s List is now a 95 on page speed and the codegen luddites should take a seat 🫡 https://t.co/XkNYlxbenM

I make tools on GStack for myself, so I am releasing /plan-devex-review today to help you create amazing Developer Experiences.

Big thanks to Addy Osmani's DX framework for the inspiration here. https://t.co/i5Qy553Q8Q
I see me tagged in this photo and I like it https://t.co/Kbxo13EogP

builderInsights.title: Nan Yu
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Design 和 PM 不应该硬绑在一起
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他的观点是，一旦 design 被塞进 product 里，探索就会变浅，品牌也会被拖累。更好的做法是让 brand/product design 保持紧密，但不要从属。 他还认为 PMM 应该归到 product management 这边，而 design + PM 这种组合会带来错误激励，把工作边界搞模糊。

tweets.text:
https://t.co/RM41VPpSP5

When design and product are jammed together, then the former ends up reporting into the latter.  

Explorations get shallow and aesthetics & brand suffer. It’s generally better to have brand and product design sit close.

Yes. PMM is a product concern. 

I see a lot of orgs out there combining design and product management, which I think creates all sorts of poor incentives.

But product management and product marketing are a much more natural fit together. https://t.co/c5z6RpznxD

https://t.co/2xXYdLA1tY

They may roll their eyes.. and then one day they will finish a project. 

And that project will have 40 incomplete issues. And they will want to group them by usecase. And for some of them, they will want to create new projects and other ones they will want to cancel or route to a different team.

And they will wonder how they’ll do this. At which point they will copy paste this tweet and tell the agent: “do that”

builderInsights.title: Swyx

builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说自己碰到了一个很奇怪的新门槛：把博客文章和 tweets 直接贴进去，Devin 就能一次性把实现做出来。关键点在于底层模型——Gemini Flash Lite——按这个任务来说其实完全不在分布范围里，但它还是跑通了。这对 agentic coding workflow 来说，是个很强的信号。

tweets.text:
you literally watch the recorded videos of the feature working after its implemented 

with annotation of whats being tested https://t.co/HrltvzE03C https://t.co/9puvHxvm8Q
this is why nobody takes ai twitter seriously https://t.co/MpTIo5aa6G

We have achieved agentic self improvement - i can just copy paste blogposts and tweets into @devinai and it oneshots the complete implementation

wasnt actually sure this was gonna work, jaw dropped when it did. this is very out of distribution of the underlying @GoogleDeepMind Gemini Flash Lite model but it Just Worked.

podcastHighlights.title: Marc Andreessen 谈 The Death of the Browser、Pi + OpenClaw，以及为什么“这次不一样”
podcastHighlights.suggestedTitle: AI 的爆发是真的，但落地过程会很乱
podcastHighlights.suggestedSummary:
**核心结论：** AI 与其说是一次 hype cycle，不如说是 80 年积压需求终于开始兑现。

- 这些大突破不是凭空冒出来的。它们是一路从 neural nets、AlexNet、transformers，叠到 reasoning、agent 和 self-improvement。
- 真正的风险不是 AI 不 work 了，而是大家按错了 scaling assumption 去重仓，像 dot-com 时代的 telecom 一样。
- 现在最难的不是 model capability，而是 messy adoption：institutions、governments、companies，还有几十亿人，不会整齐划一地往前走。

Andreessen Horowitz 的 cofounder Marc Andreessen 把 AI 看成几十年严肃研究的总爆发，不是什么突然降临的 miracle。他称之为“**eighty year overnight success**”，意思是今天这些跃迁，都是从 1943 年那篇 neural network 论文开始，一路经过 Dartmouth 时代、80 年代 expert systems 热潮、AlexNet，再到 transformers 的长期积累。核心判断很直接：**现在我们知道 neural network 才是正确的 architecture**。而最近从 chatbot 到 reasoning model，再到 coding agent 的跨越，才真正让这项技术在现实世界里“站住了”。

让他从谨慎乐观变成彻底确信的，是一连串功能上的突破。LLM 很强，但像 o1 和 r1 这样的 reasoning model，直接回答了一个质疑：这些系统到底是不是只会 autocomplete。接着，coding 这道门槛也被跨过去了——如果 AI 能和严肃 programmer 正面较量，那它就能往别的领域快速扩散。再往后，agent 和 self-improvement 让整个走势不再像 demo，而更像一次 platform shift。

不过，Andreessen 也没天真到忽略 downside。他提醒，dot-com crash 不只是 demand 的问题，更是 infrastructure 建太多了。今天也可能重演同样的事：如果资本涌进 data center 和 GPU 的速度，快过真实需求，泡沫照样会炸。只是和当年的 telecom boom 不同，现在下场的 builder 是 Microsoft、Amazon、Google、Meta、Nvidia、OpenAI 和 Anthropic。这些公司有真金白银，有真实收入，也有真实的 capacity pressure。所以即使路上会很难看，长期 upside 还是更站得住。
